county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1842 from Burke and Rutherford counties. Pre-1842 records in both parent counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and McDowell County ROD online. For pre-1842, check Burke County (severely burned 1865) and Rutherford County records.

source_profiles.source_name: McDowell County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search. Some pre-formation deeds from 1813 included.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. One of NC's best-preserved colonial counties.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Anson 1762. Parent of Tryon (1768, extinct), Cabarrus (1792), Union (1842). The Mecklenburg Declaration of Independence (May 20, 1775) and Mecklenburg Resolves (May 31, 1775) — NC's earliest revolutionary documents. Charlotte is NC's largest city.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with Mecklenburg County ROD online — deeds from 1763. FamilySearch has extensive collections. Robinson-Spangler Carolina Room at Charlotte Mecklenburg Library is an outstanding local research facility. Mecklenburg Genealogical Society is very active.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: excellent online search with images from 1763

source_profiles.source_name: Mecklenburg County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Excellent online deed search with images from 1763. One of the best ROD portals in NC.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation. Colonial-era wills significant.
Marriage bonds from 1762 and registers from 1851.

source_profiles.source_name: Robinson-Spangler Carolina Room — Charlotte Mecklenburg Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Outstanding local research facility with extensive genealogical collections, vertical files, and local history materials.

county_metadata.record_losses: Partial loss: 1907 courthouse fire damaged some records, but most survived.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1861 from five counties. Pre-1861 records scattered across Yancey, Burke, Watauga, Caldwell, and McDowell counties. Mountain county — very remote in the 19th century.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch. Mitchell County ROD has online search. For pre-1861, primarily check Yancey County records. Census from 1870.

source_profiles.source_name: Mitchell County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records.
Marriage records from county formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: Triple courthouse fires: 1835, 1840, 1886. Significant record losses. However, marriage records from 1779, probate from 1785, and court records from 1807 have survived. Incomplete deeds pre-1835; some refiled deeds available.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Anson 1779. Parent of Stanly (1841). Pre-1779 records in Anson County. NCGS article 'A Birdseye View of Montgomery County Records before 1840' is essential reading.
county_metadata.research_tips: Triple burned county requires creative research. Start with FamilySearch for surviving records. NC State Archives critical. The NCGS article on pre-1840 records is essential. Adjacent county records (Anson, Stanly, Moore) may fill gaps.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: online portal — surviving records with gaps pre-1835

source_profiles.source_name: Montgomery County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search. Incomplete pre-1835 due to fires; some refiled deeds available.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Three courthouse fires caused significant gaps.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Surviving probate records from 1785.
Marriage records surviving from county formation.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Remarkable survival for a triple-burned county.

source_profiles.source_name: NC State Archives — Montgomery County Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Critical for this triple-burned county. State Archives may have records that bypassed courthouse storage.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Cumberland 1784. Pre-1784 records in Cumberland County (excellent preservation). Parent of Lee (1907). Pinehurst/Southern Pines resort area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Moore County ROD. Cumberland County has fully digitized records from 1754 for pre-Moore research. Moore County Historical Association has local collections.

source_profiles.source_name: Moore County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed lookup from earliest records.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Edgecombe 1777. Pre-1777 records in Edgecombe County (exceptional preservation — deeds from 1732). Rocky Mount straddles Nash and Edgecombe counties.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch deeds and wills. Nash County ROD has online portal. For pre-1777, Edgecombe County has outstanding colonial records. Braswell Memorial Library Kornegay Room in Rocky Mount covers both Nash and Edgecombe.

source_profiles.source_name: Nash County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1777 and registers from 1851.
Local history room in Rocky Mount covering both Nash and Edgecombe counties. Genealogical collections and local history materials.
